comment: Default-FAIL verification contract. Checks flipped to true only after their command ran and evidence was inspected this session. Stack is shell (install.sh / install.ps1); precedent: specs/local-env-mcp/verification/T-009.contract.json (local-env-mcp's analogous high-risk shell-stack uninstall/installer task) waives lint/typecheck/build for the same reason -- check-contract.py's compile_waivable rule (COMPILE_CHECKS waivable when stack is in NONCODE_STACKS) permits this at high tier without downgrading unit-tests/acceptance-tests/regression/requirement-traceability, which all remain required:true with real evidence below. unit-tests and acceptance-tests both cite install-tests.log (35 passed / 0 failed, covering the new ci-mcp scenarios plus the pre-existing sdd-forge-mcp/local-env-mcp scenarios) and the T-009 red/green pair; red evidence was captured by reverting install.sh/install.ps1 to their pre-ci-mcp committed content while keeping the new test scenarios in place (disclosed deviation in T-009-red.txt: source edits and test-writing happened in the same working session, not literal red-first commits, but the captured red run is a genuine failing-first state against the same content the green run later exercises).
